nc_codes.name: Unconditional Program Stop
nc_codes.description: Halts spindle, coolant, and axis motion. Pressing Cycle Start resumes the program from the next block.

nc_codes.name: Optional Program Stop
nc_codes.description: Program stops only if the "Optional Stop" switch on the operator panel is ON. Otherwise, ignored.

nc_codes.name: End of Program
nc_codes.description: Stops program execution. Typically does not rewind the program pointer back to the top block.

nc_codes.name: Spindle Clockwise (CW)
nc_codes.description: Starts the spindle rotating in the forward direction.

nc_codes.name: Spindle Counter-Clockwise (CCW)
nc_codes.description: Starts the spindle rotating in the reverse direction.

nc_codes.name: Spindle Stop
nc_codes.description: Decelerates and stops spindle rotation.

nc_codes.name: Tool Change
nc_codes.description: Commands an automatic tool change (ATC) or turret index to the selected tool number.

nc_codes.name: Mist Coolant ON
nc_codes.description: Turns on secondary mist coolant system.

nc_codes.name: Flood Coolant ON
nc_codes.description: Turns on the main flood coolant pump.

nc_codes.name: Coolant OFF
nc_codes.description: Turns off all coolant pumps (flood, mist, thru-spindle).

nc_codes.name: Chuck/Vise Clamp
nc_codes.description: Commands the spindle chuck jaws or vise to clamp the workpiece (typically Lathe/Mill accessory).

nc_codes.name: Chuck/Vise Unclamp
nc_codes.description: Commands the spindle chuck jaws or vise to release/unclamp.

nc_codes.name: Spindle Orientation
nc_codes.description: Locks the spindle in a precise angular position (needed for tool changes or index operations).

nc_codes.name: End of Program and Reset
nc_codes.description: Stops spindle, coolant, and axes; resets and rewinds the program pointer back to the first block.

nc_codes.name: Subprogram Call
nc_codes.description: Calls an external subprogram. Syntax: `M98 P<program_number> L<repetitions>`

nc_codes.name: Subprogram End / Loop
nc_codes.description: Ends a subprogram and returns to the calling main program. If run in the main program, loops back to the start.
